skill.name: code-documentation-code-explain
skill.description: Explains complex code with structured walkthroughs, diagrams, examples, pitfalls, and learning guidance for developers.
skill.summary: Create clear code explanations, diagrams, and learning paths for complex implementations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 71 potential issues, but context shows the matches are Markdown documentation examples, prose, and one recommended learning URL. No executable scripts, prompt-injection attempts, command execution directives, credential access, or data exfiltration behavior were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline references used to present Python examples or link to the playbook. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands and do not provide executable install or runtime scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences, Python example boundaries, or resource references. No shell interpreter, command invocation, or user-controlled command construction is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography alerts correspond to ordinary words such as design, nodes, code, and class diagram content. No cryptographic functions, hashing APIs, encryption routines, or security-sensitive algorithms were found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are educational prose or diagram-generation examples. The surrounding text confirms there is no crypto API use and no recommendation to use weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms in Educational Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines use terms such as function map, event handling system, class diagram, and common pitfalls as part of code explanation examples. They do not collect host details, enumerate files, inspect processes, or request environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is confined to explanatory Markdown and Python snippets for teaching code concepts. No system commands, filesystem traversal, host fingerprinting, or environment reads were identified.

low_findings.title: Benign Hardcoded Learning URL
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URL is listed as a recommended external tutorial resource for async programming. It is not fetched, posted to, or used to transmit user data from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears inside a learning resources list with title, difficulty, and time estimate metadata. There is no network client or automated request path connected to the URL.

content.value_statement: Complex code is hard to teach, review, and onboard against without a clear structure. This skill creates step-by-step explanations, diagrams, examples, pitfalls, and learning paths.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes code structure, dependencies, complexity hotspots, and key concepts.
Creates high-level summaries followed by detailed walkthroughs of important components.
Produces Mermaid-style flow diagrams, class diagrams, and algorithm visualizations when useful.
Explains programming concepts with simple examples and progressive detail.
Identifies pitfalls, edge cases, and best-practice alternatives for the reviewed code.
Suggests learning paths and practice exercises based on observed concepts.
limitations: It explains existing code and does not focus on implementing new features.
Diagram quality depends on the clarity and completeness of the provided code context.
It may need extra project context to explain business rules accurately.
It does not replace security, performance, or production readiness reviews.

use_cases.title: Onboard New Engineers
use_cases.description: Create guided explanations of unfamiliar modules, control flow, and important design decisions.

use_cases.title: Teach Algorithms
use_cases.description: Break algorithms into plain-language steps with visual execution traces and key edge cases.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Reviews
use_cases.description: Summarize complex code before architecture discussions, refactor planning, or design reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Walkthrough
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner explanation of a small code section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this code for a beginner. Start with its purpose, then walk through each important step and define key terms.

prompt_templates.title: Module Onboarding Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured explanation for a new team member.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an onboarding walkthrough for this module. Include the main responsibilities, data flow, dependencies, and common mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Algorithm Visualization
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this algorithm with a step-by-step trace, a simple diagram, time complexity, space complexity, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Deep Dive
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced explanation of a complex system area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this code as an architecture deep dive. Cover component boundaries, control flow, hidden assumptions, tradeoffs, and learning resources.

output_examples.input: A developer shares an unfamiliar authentication module.
output_examples.output: The response explains the module purpose, request flow, token lifecycle, dependency roles, failure paths, and review questions.

output_examples.input: A student asks how a recursive function works.
output_examples.output: The response describes the base case, recursive step, call stack sequence, final result, and common mistakes.

output_examples.input: A team needs a design review summary for a service class.
output_examples.output: The response outlines responsibilities, collaborators, data movement, complexity hotspots, and suggested discussion points.

best_practices: Provide the relevant code, file purpose, and audience level before asking for an explanation.
Ask for diagrams when control flow, class relationships, or algorithms are central to understanding.
Request pitfalls and edge cases after the main walkthrough to support practical learning.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as the only source for security-sensitive conclusions.
Do not ask for broad explanations without sharing the relevant code or context.
Do not use it to generate implementation changes when the goal is documentation or learning.

faq.question: What type of code can this skill explain?
faq.answer: It can explain functions, classes, algorithms, modules, design patterns, and system flows when code context is provided.

faq.question: Does it write new production code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed for explanation, documentation, learning, and review preparation.

faq.question: Can it create diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create flow diagrams, class diagrams, and algorithm visualizations when they improve clarity.

faq.question: Is it useful for onboarding?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn unfamiliar code into guided walkthroughs for new developers.

faq.question: Does it need the full repository?
faq.answer: No. It can work with a focused snippet, but larger context improves explanations of dependencies and business rules.

faq.question: Which AI tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
